name: Hongshao Wanyu (Red-cooked Carp)
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Rice wine or dry sherry
1 tbsp Whole bean sauce (yellow bean sauce)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the dried tangerine peel or citrus peel for 20 minutes in warm water, or until it is soft. Rinse under running water and squeeze out any excess liquid.
2. Finely chop the soaked citrus peel and set aside.
3. Make three or four slashes on each side of the fish to help it cook faster and allow flavors to permeate. Rub salt evenly on both sides of the fish.
4. Sprinkle cornstarch evenly on each side of the fish.
5. Heat a wok or deep saute pan until hot. Add oil. When hot, deep-fry the fish on each side for 5 to 8 minutes, until brown and crispy. Remove fish and drain on paper towels.
6. Pour off most of the oil, leaving about 2 tablespoons in the wok.
7. Reheat the wok. Add chopped citrus peel, garlic, ginger, and scallions. Stir-fry for 30 seconds.
8. Add the remaining ingredients (salt, cornstarch, peanut oil, minced ginger, scallions, rice wine or dry sherry, whole bean sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, and chicken stock or water) to the wok.
9. Return the fish to the wok, spooning the ingredients over the top of the fish.
10. Cover the wok and cook over low heat for 8 minutes.
11. Serve immediately. Serves 4 as part of a Chinese meal, or 2 as a single dish.
